hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models trained on Materials Project and OQMD data produce candidate crystal structures up to 64 atoms that are then relaxed with DFT, then a substantial fraction of these candidates will exhibit low RMSD after relaxation, pass structural validity checks (e.g., minimum interatomic distances >0.5 Å), and have formation energies within 0.1 eV/atom of the convex hull, demonstrating that the models can generate chemically plausible and thermodynamically stable materials.
